rationale: Both correctly diagnose the same root cause (missing extended ratio tokens on the Base tokenizer, regression from #2713 forcing two-stage topology) and give the same confirmed workaround, matching the thread closely. Y is more complete: it explains *why* the config choice matters (AR-stage vs DiT-only topology), supplies concrete fallback code for the guard rather than just describing it, and correctly captures the actual resolution (issue effectively resolved via workaround/docs, improvement tracked in a new issue per Gaohan123) — whereas X's structured 'disposition: keep-open' field is inconsistent with that outcome even though its prose mentions the same request. Grounding is comparable, with Y citing slightly more specific (if unverifiable) evidence from the yaml headers.
